decision_scope: 2D interpretation proposal only; no corrected source, Step 10 revision, world construction frame, or geometry

reference_policy.X: Use the C-001 120 cm annotation span as the unchanged review reference; compute only the C-004 whole-component X factor needed to express 140/120 in the same review frame.
reference_policy.Y: Use the C-001 90 cm annotation span as the unchanged review reference; compute only the C-004 whole-component Y factor needed to express 110/90 in the same review frame.
reference_policy.Z: Use the complete-asset 220 cm annotation span as the unchanged review reference; compute only the C-004 whole-component Z factor needed to express 35/220 in the same review frame.
reference_policy.reference_limit: Annotation spans are approximate target-layout evidence and review coordinates, not geometry boundaries, centers, or historical world-scale calibrators.

complete_asset: one global Z review normalization to the 220 cm target
C_001: one axis-aligned X scale proposal if required
one axis-aligned Y scale proposal if required
C_002: rigid translation only when required to preserve an approved visible contact
C_003: rigid translation only when required to preserve an approved visible contact

blocked_operations: local outer-only or inner-only C-004 deformation
center, origin, pivot, snap anchor, topology, or geometry creation

required_whole_C004_factors.formula: factor = (target C-004 to reference target ratio) / (source annotation-span ratio)

contact_preservation_rule.required: Every approved visible contact sample used by the proposal must remain coincident after the permitted component operations.
contact_preservation_rule.C_003_limit: C-003 may receive only one rigid translation, so every transformed C-004 CL-003 sample must demand the same translation vector.
contact_preservation_rule.feasibility_test: For two CL-003 samples p_i and p_j and C-004 scale S, translation cancels: required displacement difference = (S-I)(p_i-p_j). If this is nonzero for any pair, one rigid C-003 translation cannot preserve both contacts.
